papers.title: Energy inflation in the shadow of US–China tensions: Is the energy transition a pathway to stability?
papers.authors: Ebrahim Merza, Ilhan Ozturk
papers.abstract: Investigates the influence of US-China tensions and energy transition on energy inflation using MMQR for 33 countries (1995-2023). Results show US-China tensions increase energy inflation across all quantiles, while energy transition mitigates it at lower and medium levels. Technology advancement and globalization reduce energy inflation across all quantiles.
notes: 核心方法: MMQR (Method of Moments Quantile Regression)
自變數：US-China Tensions, Energy Transition, EG, FD, TD, UP, GLOB
美中緊張在全部quantile都顯著推升能源通膨
能源轉型在低至中quantile顯著抑制通膨
技術發展與全球化在所有quantile都降低能源成本

papers.title: Artificial intelligence and natural resource exploitation shaping load capacity factor in Quad economies: Role of renewable energy and green innovations
papers.authors: Yang Cui, Muhammad Usman, Muhammad Irfan, Mohammad Haseeb
papers.abstract: Examines heterogeneous effects of AI adoption and natural resource exploitation on load capacity factor (LCF) in Quad economies (US, Japan, India, Australia) from 1995-2023 using MMQR. AI negatively impacts LCF (−0.240 to −0.305 across quantiles). Natural resources (+0.305 to +0.330), green innovation (+0.093 to +0.249), financial development (+1.033 to +1.406, strongest), and renewable energy (+0.421 to +0.764) all positively affect LCF.
notes: 核心方法: MMQR (Method of Moments Quantile Regression)
因變數: Load Capacity Factor (LCF) = 生物承載力/生態足跡
AI採用在所有quantile都顯著惡化LCF, 高LCF時破壞更大
自然資源開採對LCF為正向(需搭配妥善投資)
金融發展是影響LCF最強的變數
再生能源效益隨LCF水準提升而擴大
